summary: CC-0099 restricts the mode on mounted Fernet/credential key Secret volumes to silence Keystone's world-readable key_repository warning. Adds Pod-level SecurityContext.FSGroup=openstackUID and DefaultMode=0o400 on both fernet-keys and credential-keys Secret volumes in (a) the API Deployment, (b) the fernet rotation CronJob, and (c) the credential rotation CronJob — symmetric on both rotation paths including the cross-mounted read-only counterpart Secret. The rotation CronJob init container now uses 'install -m 0400 ...' instead of 'cp ...' so the EmptyDir working copy keeps the non-world-readable mode after the kubelet's per-process write. 14 new unit tests assert FSGroup, DefaultMode 0o400, init-container mode argv, container-VolumeMounts regression guard, and CC-0045 container-level SecurityContext invariance. New chainsaw E2E suite tests/e2e/keystone/key-repository-mode/ scrapes the keystone container log for ZERO matches of the world-readable warning on each path independently. Docs updated with fsGroup/defaultMode rows for the API Pod and both rotation CronJob volume tables, plus _check_key_repository rationale.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests added for Deployment FSGroup + DefaultMode (4 tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go)
Unit tests added for fernet rotation CronJob FSGroup + DefaultMode + init-container argv (5 tests)
Unit tests added for credential rotation CronJob FSGroup + DefaultMode + init-container argv (5 tests)
All new tests pass: go test ./internal/controller/... -run 'CC0099|FSGroup|...' clean
Chainsaw E2E test asserts zero matches for fernet path and credential path independently (separate grep invocations)
Container-level SecurityContext (CC-0045) regression guards in place via expectRestrictedSecurityContext
VolumeMounts regression guards on rotate containers via ConsistOf assertion
Existing chainsaw rotation tests (fernet-rotation, credential-rotation) untouched in this diff
Edge case: scope-guard tests assert config ConfigMap DefaultMode remains unset
Edge case: scope-guard tests assert scripts ConfigMap DefaultMode remains 0o555 (CC-0073)

code_quality_checklist.item: No new helper introduced for the one-field PodSecurityContext literal — inline at all 3 call sites
openstackUID constant reused; no hardcoded 42424 literal in implementation (only appears in test failure messages as commentary)
No new imports introduced — corev1 and ptr already imported in all three files; strings imported in test files only (already present)
Comments explain WHY (FSGroup rationale, install -m 0400 rationale) not WHAT
Feature ID CC-0099 referenced in every new comment, test failure message, doc row, and chainsaw error message
Consistent symmetric structure between fernet and credential rotation CronJob changes
go vet clean on the diff
Pre-existing golangci-lint findings (errorlint in rotation_validation.go, staticcheck on result.Requeue in reconcile_credential_test.go) are out of scope — not introduced by this PR

security_checklist.item: Secret volume DefaultMode tightened from default 0o644 to 0o400 (owner read-only) on every Fernet/credential key volume
Pod-level FSGroup applied so the openstack UID/GID can read its own keys without exposing them group/world-readable
EmptyDir working copy in rotation Pods materialised at mode 0o400 via 'install -m 0400' — no world-readable transient state during rotation
No secrets exposed in process arguments, logs, or environment
No regression on container-level PSS Restricted profile (RunAsNonRoot, ReadOnlyRootFilesystem, etc.)
No new attack surface: FSGroup is a Pod-level chown/chmod by kubelet, doesn't relax any existing constraint
Defence-in-depth: fixes the symmetric credential path that was previously masked by the fernet warning firing first

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is the simplest possible — three small inline literal changes plus init-container command swap
Follows existing inline-PodSpec construction style; no premature abstraction (no helper for one-field PodSecurityContext)
Container-level SecurityContext (CC-0045) and Pod-level FSGroup remain orthogonal — neither overrides the other
FSGroupChangePolicy left at default (Always) — documented as intentional for in-place rotation correctness
Docs match implementation: Deployment volume table, both rotation CronJob volume tables, and front-matter feature list all updated

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No code duplication: same PodSecurityContext literal at 3 call sites is acceptable per task plan (no helper extraction)

Tests duplicate the same assertion structure across three call sites (Deployment, fernet CronJob, credential CronJob) — pattern of test mirroring is established by existing CC-0073/CC-0045 tests in the same files
Init container command is the minimal viable change: 'cp' → 'install -m 0400' (one token swap plus mode flag)
No speculative future-proofing: FSGroupChangePolicy explicitly left unset

fail_fast_checklist.item: Test failure messages embed CC-0099 and the specific assertion intent
Chainsaw script uses 'set -euo pipefail' and emits CC-0099 REQ-006 tagged error messages on regression
Chainsaw assertions are independent (separate grep invocations per path) so a regression on either path fails alone with a path-specific error message

defensive_checklist.item: Pod-level RunAs* / Seccomp / SELinux / AppArmor explicitly asserted nil — preserves CC-0045 container-level contract
Scope-guard tests assert config and scripts volumes are NOT modified by CC-0099
VolumeMounts ConsistOf assertion locks in the rotate-container mount layout against future drift
Init container Command argv inspected at index 2 (sh -c <cmd>) with explicit length guard
Chainsaw catch block dumps pod logs, describes, kubelet-projected volume modes (ls -la), and namespace events on failure for fast diagnosis

issues_found.description: Comment claim 'upstream Keystone refuses to start otherwise' on the FSGroup rationale in both rotation CronJob builders is slightly inaccurate. Upstream Keystone LOGS a WARNING via fernet_utils._check_key_repository when the key directory is world-readable; it does not refuse to start. The actual operational reason FSGroup is needed here is that without it the kubelet projects Secret files as owned by root:root, and a 0o400 mode would prevent the openstack UID (42424) from reading its own keys. The comment slightly conflates the world-readable WARNING (the user-visible problem the feature targets) with a hard startup failure.
issues_found.fix: Reword the inline comment to: '// FSGroup makes the kubelet group-own mounted Secret volumes by the openstack GID so DefaultMode 0o400 still lets the openstack UID read the keys via the group bit. Without it, kubelet would project files as root:root mode 0o400, the openstack process could not read them, and upstream Keystone would log a `key_repository is world readable` WARNING for any default-mode (0o644) workaround (CC-0099).'

suggested_improvements: Consider extending the chainsaw key-repository-mode test to also exec into the API pod and `ls -la /etc/keystone/fernet-keys/` and `/etc/keystone/credential-keys/` and grep -v for `r--r--r--` (world-read bit) — this would catch a kubelet projection regression even if upstream Keystone changes the warning text. The existing test only checks the upstream warning string. (Not required for APPROVED — current coverage matches REQ-006 verbatim.)

Consider adding a one-line chainsaw assertion that scrapes the keystone container log for `OWNER MISMATCH` from _check_key_repository (which fires when the directory owner is root but the process runs as a non-root user). With FSGroup set the directory is chowned to root:42424 — upstream's check is a true world-readable bit test (S_IROTH), not an owner check, so this is theoretical; useful as belt-and-braces if upstream tightens the check in the future. (Not required.)
next_steps: Optional: apply the AUTO_FIX rewording for the rotation-CronJob FSGroup comment to remove the 'refuses to start' inaccuracy.

Optional follow-up after merge: schedule a periodic agent to re-scrape keystone logs in production for any new world-readable warnings from upstream code paths the chainsaw test does not cover (e.g., expand_keys, validate_keys utility commands).
